scope: Founder, investor, funding-round, advisory-vs-equity, and ownership-control intelligence.
method: Extracted deterministic investor/control facts from collected evidence documents.
Merged curated public-source notes for investor/funding cases not yet present in data_02 documents.
Explicitly marks ownership percentages as unknown unless a source states them.

records.ownership_basis: Not disclosed in collected public evidence.
records.rm_impact_reason: Executive/control person is in the KYC baseline; equity ownership is not captured here.
records.recommended_action: Screen control persons and request ownership percentage if they hold founder, board, or voting-control rights.

records.ownership_basis: Official announcement confirms acquisition close; consideration and final ownership percentage are not in the collected excerpt.
records.rm_impact_reason: Adds institutional crypto exchange capabilities and new licensed entities, creating custody/trading opportunities and ownership-control KYC work.
records.recommended_action: Request post-close corporate structure, Bitstamp legal-entity ownership chart, and whether consideration was cash, equity, or mixed.
records.evidence_quote: Robinhood Completes Acquisition of Bitstamp Robinhood Markets, Inc.

top_rm_questions: Who owns what percentage today, and are any stakes voting/control rights rather than passive economics?

records.ownership_basis: Not disclosed in collected public evidence.
records.rm_impact_reason: Investor was present in the last baseline, but ownership percentage and investor rights are not captured.
records.recommended_action: Request updated investor schedule and distinguish passive equity, advisory role, board rights, and control rights.

records.ownership_basis: Not disclosed in collected public evidence.
records.rm_impact_reason: Executive/control person is in the KYC baseline; equity ownership is not captured here.
records.recommended_action: Screen control persons and request ownership percentage if they hold founder, board, or voting-control rights.

records.ownership_basis: Official ICE release discloses investment amount and valuation, but not percentage ownership.
records.rm_impact_reason: Strategic investment from NYSE parent improves institutional credibility, data distribution, and tokenization partnership potential, while preserving regulatory-review needs.
records.recommended_action: Treat as strong commercial signal; ask for cap table update, investor rights, data-distribution terms, and any regulatory commitments tied to ICE.
records.evidence_quote: ICE will invest up to $2 billion in Polymarket

records.ownership_basis: Secondary report says ICE invested for around a 20% stake.
records.rm_impact_reason: A large strategic stake can materially change governance expectations, institutional distribution, and future banking needs.
records.recommended_action: Verify percentage ownership from primary company confirmation or updated corporate records before treating as beneficial ownership.

records.entity_name: Founders Fund; Polychain Capital; General Catalyst; 1789 Capital
records.ownership_basis: Investor names and prior funding amount reported; individual ownership percentages not disclosed.
records.rm_impact_reason: High-quality crypto/VC backers are commercially positive; politically exposed or regulatory-sensitive investors may raise reputational review questions.
records.recommended_action: Ask customer to classify investor rights, board/advisory seats, side letters, and whether any investors are passive, advisory, or controlling.
records.evidence_quote: Backers include Founders Fund, Polychain Capital, General Catalyst, and 1789 Capital.

records.ownership_basis: Official release identifies founder and CEO, but does not disclose founder ownership.
records.rm_impact_reason: Founder-led status is relevant for control-person screening and relationship context; ownership percentage remains unverified.
records.recommended_action: Screen founder/control person and request current beneficial ownership or control-person schedule.
records.evidence_quote: Shayne Coplan, Founder and CEO of Polymarket

top_rm_questions: Who owns what percentage today, and are any stakes voting/control rights rather than passive economics?
Which named investors are equity holders, advisors, board observers, or purely commercial partners?
Did the round add board seats, veto rights, side letters, token warrants, or information rights?
Does the investor/advisor profile create reputational, PEP, sanctions, or regulatory sensitivity?

records.ownership_basis: Not disclosed in collected public evidence.
records.rm_impact_reason: Executive/control person is in the KYC baseline; equity ownership is not captured here.

records.ownership_basis: Filing permits future debt/equity financing for Bitcoin purchases, but no named investor or stake is disclosed.
records.rm_impact_reason: Future issuances could dilute shareholders or increase leverage while funding digital-asset treasury exposure.
records.recommended_action: Monitor financing announcements, noteholder identities if public, dilution, covenants, and custody banking needs.
records.evidence_quote: GameStop Form 10-K for fiscal year 2024 On March 25, 2025 we announced that, as part of our revisions to the Investment Policy, the Board approved the addition of Bitcoin as a treasury reserve asset, whereby a portion of our cash or future debt and equity issuances may be invested in Bitcoin.

records.ownership_basis: Secondary profile says Cohen's GameStop stake was 8.4% as of April 2025; primary proxy/13D verification is required.
records.rm_impact_reason: Founder-like activist control person with material equity stake can drive strategy, financing, and acquisition behavior.
records.recommended_action: Verify current stake in SEC proxy/13D filings and ask whether RC Ventures has board/control rights or related-party exposure.
records.evidence_quote: Cohen's stake in Gamestop has decreased ... to 8.4%

records.ownership_basis: Official source discloses acquisition price, not seller ownership or final cap-table effects.
records.rm_impact_reason: Material strategic M&A expands Kraken into U.S. retail futures and multi-asset trading, increasing financing, treasury, and regulatory needs.
records.recommended_action: Ask for acquisition funding source, seller rollover equity if any, post-close subsidiary structure, and regulatory approvals.
records.evidence_quote: Kraken to Acquire NinjaTrader: Introducing the Next Era of Professional Trading Kraken has entered into an agreement to acquire NinjaTrader, the leading U.S.

records.ownership_basis: Product disclosure says holders do not own underlying shares.
records.rm_impact_reason: Clarifies that product users get economic exposure rather than shareholder control; still relevant for securities, suitability, and custody risk.
records.recommended_action: Do not treat xStocks holders as shareholders of underlying companies; review issuer, distributor, custody, and client-suitability obligations.
records.evidence_quote: Holders of xStocks do not have ownership in any of the underlying stock or shares of the companies to which they are economically linked ("underlying stock"); instead, by purchasing xStocks investors can benefit from exposure to the performance of the underlying stock.

records.ownership_basis: Investor-relations page confirms public-company monitoring source, but does not disclose principal shareholders in the collected text.
records.rm_impact_reason: Public-company status improves filing availability and enables systematic shareholder/proxy monitoring.
records.recommended_action: Collect latest S-1/424B4/proxy principal-stockholder table to identify founder and institutional ownership percentages.
records.evidence_quote: Circle Investor Relations Circle is a global financial technology firm that enables businesses of all sizes to harness the power of digital currencies and public blockchains for payments, commerce, and financial applications worldwide.

records.entity_name: Jeremy Allaire; Jim Breyer; Accel Partners; General Catalyst Partners
records.ownership_basis: Secondary profile identifies founder and seed backers; current post-IPO ownership percentages are not captured.
records.rm_impact_reason: High-quality fintech/VC seed backing is commercially positive, but current public-company control requires S-1/proxy verification.
records.recommended_action: Collect Circle S-1/424B4 principal-stockholder table and distinguish founder ownership from institutional float.
records.evidence_quote: $9 million Series A funding from Jim Breyer, Accel Partners, and General Catalyst Partners

top_rm_questions: Who owns what percentage today, and are any stakes voting/control rights rather than passive economics?

records.ownership_basis: RDAP links domain eligibility to Coinbase identity data; it is not equity ownership evidence.
records.rm_impact_reason: Country-code domain control may indicate market-entry preparation, but it is not proof of active operations or beneficial ownership.
records.recommended_action: Ask whether the domain is defensive, pre-launch, or tied to Australian operating activity; do not treat as cap-table evidence.
records.evidence_quote: RDAP record for coinbase.au RDAP for coinbase.au lists registrant name Coinbase, Inc., eligibility type Trademark Owner, eligibility name COINBASE, eligibility id TM 1647712, and last changed 2026-02-09T01:44:15.302Z.

records.ownership_basis: Secondary public profile lists Brian Armstrong as owner at 19%; primary proxy verification is required.
records.rm_impact_reason: Founder/CEO with large ownership can align strategy but also concentrates control-person and reputational exposure.
records.recommended_action: Verify beneficial ownership and voting power from the latest Coinbase proxy statement before updating KYC.

records.ownership_basis: Secondary profile reports IPO-era stake; current stake requires SEC verification.
records.rm_impact_reason: Co-founder/director equity is relevant for control-person screening but current economic exposure may have changed.
records.recommended_action: Collect latest director beneficial ownership and related-party data from Coinbase SEC filings.

records.ownership_basis: Secondary shareholder profile reports Class B share ownership; current proxy should verify exact voting power.
records.rm_impact_reason: Class B super-voting shares create founder control despite lower economic ownership, relevant for governance-risk context.

records.evidence_quote: Larry Page ... own 45% ... of class B shares

records.ownership_percent: Vanguard 8.88% Class A / 7.64% Class C; BlackRock 7.36% Class A / 6.63% Class C
records.ownership_basis: Secondary shareholder profile reports public-share class holdings; voting influence differs by share class.
records.rm_impact_reason: Large passive institutional holders matter for governance context but do not usually imply operational control.
records.recommended_action: Treat as shareholder context, not beneficial-owner drift, unless filings show activism, concentrated voting, or related-party exposure.
